emails.subject: ERA_20260417 — Ready for Processing
emails.body_text:
Hi Maria,

Just a heads-up — ERA_20260417.xlsx came in from last week and is sitting in the queue for you.

Jason

emails.subject: Ops Check-In — Week of April 20
emails.body_text:
Team,

Quick check-in for the week. A few open items to be aware of:

- We have a handful of ERA batches from recent weeks still moving through the queue. Please make sure your assigned items are actioned before EOD Friday.
- Jason, any update on the BlueCross TX resubmission from March? Let me know if you need me to follow up on our end.
- Nothing else pressing from my side. Come find me if anything needs escalation.

David Park
RCM Manager — Pathfinder Billing and Coding

emails.subject: UnitedHealthcare Provider Portal — Unplanned Service Interruption Notice
emails.body_text:
Dear UnitedHealthcare Provider,

We are writing to notify you of an unplanned service interruption affecting the UnitedHealthcare Provider Portal and EDI submission gateway.

Impacted Services:
  - Electronic claim (837P/837I) submission queue
  - Electronic Remittance Advice (835) retrieval
  - Real-time eligibility verification

Outage Start: March 5, 2026 at 02:00 AM Central Time
Estimated Restoration: March 7, 2026 (subject to update)

Claims submitted via EDI during this window will be queued and processed upon restoration. Submission timestamps will reflect the actual received date; no timeliness penalties will apply to claims held in queue during the interruption window.

Please do not attempt duplicate submissions. We will send a follow-up notification once services are fully restored.

If you have urgent eligibility questions during the outage, please contact Provider Services at 1-800-842-4844.

Thank you for your patience.

UnitedHealthcare Provider Relations
providerrelations@uhcprovider.com

emails.subject: Escalation Notice: ESC-2026-0007 — Operational / Payer System Outage
emails.body_text:
Hi David,

I have logged ESC-2026-0007 in the Escalation Log.

Escalation Details:
  Escalation ID:       ESC-2026-0007
  Category:            Operational
  Reason:              Payer System Outage
  Payer:               UnitedHealthcare (UNHC1)
  Date:                03/05/2026
  Impact:              UHC's EDI submission gateway has been down since approximately
                       02:00 AM CT. We have 3 claims currently queued for 837P
                       submission to UHC that cannot be transmitted.

Requesting direction: should I hold the submission queue and wait for restoration, or attempt an alternate submission pathway?

Best,
Maria Santos
Resolution Auditor — Pathfinder Billing and Coding

emails.subject: Re: Escalation Notice: ESC-2026-0007 — Operational / Payer System Outage
emails.body_text:
Maria,

Received. Hold the submission queue — do not attempt an alternate pathway. UHC has confirmed this is a widespread outage affecting all providers; secondary submission attempts risk duplicates when the main gateway restores.

I'll monitor UHC communications. Once the portal is back up and we confirm queued submissions have processed, I'll update ESC-2026-0007 to Resolved and notify the team.

David

emails.subject: ESC-2026-0007 Resolved — UHC Portal Restored, Queued Submissions Cleared
emails.body_text:
Team,

UnitedHealthcare confirmed portal restoration as of March 9 at 11:00 PM CT. All 3 queued 837P submissions processed successfully. EDI acknowledgments (999/TA1) received for all claims.

I've updated ESC-2026-0007 to Resolved in the Escalation Log.

No further action needed.

David

emails.subject: Escalation Notice: ESC-2026-0003 — Financial / High Value Claim Dispute
emails.body_text:
Hi David,

I have logged ESC-2026-0003 in the Escalation Log.

Urgent Escalation: 01/22/2026
Claim Value: $7,200
Payer: Aetna (AETN1)
Issue: Denied for CO-45; payer processed claim at a reduced allowed amount that does not align with the contracted rate.
Action Taken: Reviewed claim register and fee schedule; contracted rate confirmed.
Request: Awaiting your direction before submitting the formal appeal to Aetna.

Best,
Maria Santos
Resolution Auditor — Pathfinder Billing and Coding

emails.subject: Re: Escalation Notice: ESC-2026-0003 — Financial / High Value Claim Dispute
emails.body_text:
Maria,

Good news — I escalated this directly to our Aetna Provider Relations contact. Aetna's billing team reviewed and confirmed the reduction was a system error on their end (incorrect fee schedule version applied during adjudication). They have reprocessed the claim at the correct contracted rate.

Payment correction should post within the next few business days. I've updated ESC-2026-0003 to Resolved in the Escalation Log.

Good catch on the rate mismatch.

David

emails.subject: Electronic Claim Receipt Acknowledgment — CLM-2026-0267
emails.body_text:
Dear Pathfinder Billing and Coding,

This is an automated acknowledgment confirming receipt of the following electronic claim submission:

  Payer Claim Reference:    BCBSTX-2026-044721
  Your Claim ID:            CLM-2026-0267
  Original Claim ID:        CLM-2026-0234
  Claim Type:               837P Professional (Corrected — Frequency Code 7)
  Rendering Provider:       Dr. Sarah Chen, NPI 1234567890
  Date of Service:          01/15/2026
  Received Date:            03/10/2026
  Current Status:           Received — Pending Adjudication

Expected processing time is 15–20 business days from the received date. An Explanation of Benefits (EOB) or Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) will be issued upon adjudication.

If you have questions regarding this submission, please reference payer claim number BCBSTX-2026-044721 when contacting Provider Operations at 1-800-521-2227 (Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM–7:00 PM CT).

Thank you,
BlueCross BlueShield of Texas — Provider Operations
provideroperations@bcbstx.com
